description: A capstone fight that brings the whole Tactical Crucible together. Your goal: cut down the BANDIT CAPTAIN while keeping the wounded SCOUT alive — killing the rabble alone won't win, and if the scout dies you lose. The enemies fight to ROLES: the skeleton archer (artillery) snipes your weakest — the scout — and kites; the bugbear (brute) hunts the most-wounded; the goblin (skirmisher) harries; and the captain (leader) holds their nerve — the others won't break while it stands. On round 2 the captain whistles up REINFORCEMENTS; on round 3 a toppled brazier sets the floor alight — a SPREADING FIRE you can herd them into. Drop the captain and their MORALE collapses: the survivors flee or throw down their arms (and curse, plead, and rasp the whole way — combat BARKS). Protect the scout, kill the captain, mind the fire.
customIntroduction: The waystation yard is a death-trap waiting to happen: a smoldering brazier over an oil-slicked floor, a bandit captain barking orders from the center, a skeleton archer perched at the back with an arrow already drawn, a bugbear cracking its knuckles, and a goblin slinking along the flank. Beside you, a wounded Bureau scout can barely stand — and the archer's empty eyes have already found them.
customContext:
DEMO — TACTICAL CRUCIBLE CAPSTONE. This single combat exercises every piece of the encounter-engagement set. The ENGINE drives the mechanics — narrate what the Event Log reports; do not override targets, flight, surrender, or hazard damage.

OBJECTIVE (#31): the encounter is completeOnFlagOnly — VICTORY fires only when the CAPTAIN (`bandit_captain`) dies (the `captain_down` trigger sets the flag). DEFEAT fires if the SCOUT (`villager`, your ally) dies (`scout_lost` → fail_encounter). Killing the minions does NOT end it.

ENEMY ROLES (#35): skeleton = ARTILLERY (snipes the weakest — the scout — and kites out of melee); bugbear = BRUTE (charges the most-wounded to finish it); goblin = SKIRMISHER (harries at range); bandit captain = LEADER (anchors morale). Teach the player to prioritise the archer to save the scout.

REINFORCEMENTS (#33): round 2, the captain whistles and two more bandits charge in.

HAZARD (#32): round 3, the brazier topples — a SPREADING fire (2d6, DEX save DC 13 for half) erupts mid-yard and grows each round, burning friend and foe. Keep clear; herd or SHOVE bandits into it (resolve the shove, then move_entity them onto a burning tile).

MORALE (#34) + BARKS (US-132): while the captain lives the squad holds. The moment the captain falls, morale COLLAPSES — wounded bandits flee toward the edge or throw down their arms and yield (offer the player capture/mercy). Bandits taunt, curse, and plead automatically; voice them.

NARRATE the drama — the archer fixing on the scout, the fire racing across the oil, the captain's orders, and the rout when it dies. Everything is engine-driven; you provide the voice.
objective: Cut down the bandit captain and keep the wounded scout alive. Watch the archer snipe your scout (kill it first!), reinforcements arrive on round 2, fire erupt on round 3 — and the bandits break the moment the captain falls.

then.message: DEMO (Tactical Crucible): kill the CAPTAIN to win, keep the SCOUT alive or lose. Roles — skeleton snipes your scout & kites, bugbear hunts the wounded, captain anchors morale. Round 2: reinforcements. Round 3: spreading fire. Drop the captain → the rest break.
The captain whistles — two more bandits charge in from the gate!

then.text: The brazier topples — burning oil races across the floor!
The captain falls — and the bandits' nerve goes with them.

then.reason: The scout is dead — your charge is lost.
